channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US-Iran talks in Pakistan end without agreement after 21 hours
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance announced that the US delegation is leaving Pakistan after failing to reach a peace agreement with Iran following 21 hours of negotiations. The talks, held in Islamabad, rep...

item.title: Deadly stampede at Haiti’s Laferriere Citadel leaves at least 30 dead amid annual celebrations
item.description: Authorities in Haiti reported that a devastating stampede at the historic Laferriere Citadel in the country’s northern region left at least 30 people dead on Saturday, with the death toll expected to ...

item.title: Flipkart and Amazon Expand Aggressively, Increasing Competition in India’s Quick Commerce Market
item.description: India's quick commerce market is experiencing rapid growth, driven by rising consumer demand for fast deliveries. Flipkart, owned by Walmart, entered the sector later than local competitors like Blink...

item.title: US-Iran talks in Islamabad end without agreement; Pakistan praised for mediation efforts
item.description: After 21 hours of intensive negotiations in Islamabad, the United States failed to secure a deal with Iran, US Vice President JD Vance announced. Despite a marathon series of talks, the two countries ...

item.title: FG and Nestlé Collaborate to Improve Dairy Farming and Livestock Skills
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don join hand with Nestlé Nigeria Plc to sign Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) wey go help improve livestock genetics and boost farmers' productivity. Minister of Li...

item.title: Malacañang journalists clarify independence amid claims of Palace influence
item.description: The Malacañang Press Corps (MPC), composed of journalists covering activities at the Presidential Palace, has strongly denied allegations that its members are being used or paid by the Office of the P...

item.title: Early Cracks on Ngong–Suswa Road Raise Safety and Quality Concerns
item.description: The Sh4 billion Ngong–Suswa road, recently declared complete, is already showing visible cracks just weeks after opening. The 70-kilometre road, designed to ease traffic from Nairobi–Mai Mahiu, begins...

item.title: Bank of Canada and Top Lenders Discuss Cybersecurity Risks from Anthropic AI
item.description: The Bank of Canada recently convened a meeting with major financial institutions to discuss potential cybersecurity risks associated with Anthropic AI technologies. The discussion highlighted the grow...

item.title: Blue Jays fall 7-4 to Twins as George Springer exits with toe injury
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays suffered a 7-4 defeat to the Minnesota Twins at the Rogers Centre, with the game swinging decisively during a seven-run third inning by the visitors. Trevor Larnach hit a three-r...

item.title: Bryson DeChambeau Misses the Cut as 2026 Masters Line Set at Four Over Par
item.description: The 90th Masters Tournament at Augusta National reached the halfway mark on Friday with several big names missing the cut. Bryson DeChambeau suffered a stunning finish, making a triple bogey on the 18...

item.title: US and Iran urged to maintain ceasefire after Islamabad peace talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar underlined the importance of the United States and Iran continuing to honour their ceasefire commitments, following the conclusion of th...

item.title: Lily Collins reveals long-standing ribcage tattoo at PaleyFest LA 2026
item.description: At the PaleyFest LA 2026, Lily Collins, 37, showcased her rarely seen ribcage tattoo, a delicate Tinkerbell-style design perched on a crescent moon, holding a dandelion with the words 'fragile thing' ...

item.title: Orthodox Easter Ceasefire Collapses Amid Renewed Attacks
item.description: Ukraine and Russia’s brief Easter ceasefire has failed, with Kyiv reporting nearly 470 violations by Russian forces, including air strikes, drone attacks, and shelling. The truce was intended to last ...

item.title: Diamonds and Pearls Travels Highlights Africa as a Prime Travel Destination
item.description: Plenty people dey waka enter Africa dey under-rate am, because dem don dey hear plenty old stereotypes wey no true. Diamonds and Pearls Travels wey dey Lekki, Lagos dey try change the matter by curati...

item.title: Artemis II astronauts return to Earth after historic lunar flyby
item.description: The Artemis II crew has returned to Earth following a historic lunar flyby, marking the first human journey beyond the Moon since Apollo 17 in 1972. The four astronauts—commander Reid Wiseman, pilot V...

item.title: Sebastian Duterte appointed as PDP-Laban president during party council meeting
item.description: Davao City Mayor Sebastian “Baste” Duterte has officially taken on a bigger role in national politics after being appointed as the president of the Partido Demokratiko Pilipino–Lakas ng Bayan (PDP-Lab...

item.title: This Week’s Key Developments: Artemis II Return, Middle East Crisis, Sara Duterte Impeachment, and Flood Control Updates
item.description: This week’s news highlights cover both international and local issues impacting the Philippines. The Artemis II astronauts have safely returned to Earth after their mission around the Moon, offering a...

item.title: Kakamega Family Searches for Missing Daughter with Special Needs
item.description: In Musoli, Kakamega County, a family is in deep worry after their daughter, Connic Busieka, went missing on April 3, 2026. Connic, who has a medical condition affecting her mental state, was last seen...

item.title: Call Centre Facing CCMA Claim Over Restrictive Bathroom Break Policies
item.description: A former employee of Alteram Solutions, a company operating call centres for several state agencies including the Unemployment Insurance Fund (UIF) and the Road Accident Fund, has taken the company to...

item.title: Arsenal slip in Premier League race as Bayern set Bundesliga scoring record
item.description: Arsenal’s Premier League title ambitions took a significant blow on Saturday as they fell 2-1 to Bournemouth, allowing Manchester City to close the gap in the race. Mikel Arteta labelled the defeat a ...
